title: Topological Sort
description: Given a directed acyclic graph (DAG), return a valid topological ordering of its nodes using Kahn's algorithm (BFS with in-degrees).
key_concept: Process nodes with no incoming edges first. Decrement neighbor in-degrees as nodes are removed. If order length < n → cycle detected.
when_best: Course scheduling, build order, task dependencies, detecting cycles in directed graphs

hints.L1: Compute in-degree for every node. Add all nodes with in-degree 0 to a queue. Process queue: add to result, decrement neighbors' in-degrees.
hints.L2: When a neighbor's in-degree reaches 0, add it to the queue. Continue until queue empty. If result.length < n, graph has a cycle.
hints.L3: in_deg=[0]*n; for u,v in edges: in_deg[v]++; q=deque(i for i if in_deg[i]==0); order=[]; while q: u=popleft; order.append(u); for v in graph[u]: in_deg[v]--; if in_deg[v]==0: q.append(v); return order if len==n else []
